observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accounting Office traces the entries made in the Government Equity account in the Trust Fund and prepare adjusting journal entry to correct the misstatement from prior years pursuant to the applicable accounting standards.

The Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances of reciprocal accounts and prepare necessary adjusting entries thereof. Any balances which require more time to reconcile like inherited balances shall be disclosed in the notes to the financial statements.

The Municipal Accounting Office reconciles available records with available documents and identify the particular outstanding cash advance to facilitate query to the Commission on Audit’s Intelligence and Confidential Funds Audit Office (ICFAO) on the status of liquidation if found that there really was and if there’s none, demand liquidation or proof of liquidation whichever may apply from the concerned official.

The management provides resources for the revision/updating of the Local Revenue Code in accordance with Republic Act 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991 and coordinate with the DILG, DOF and BLGF in the proper imposition of taxes and fees.

The the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator facilitate the evaluation of the status of implementation of the funds received from National Government Agencies and other LGUs.

The management submits the liquidation of all implemented projects that remained unliquidated. 

Further, the management returns the balances of those liquidated financial assistance to the Bureau of Treasury.

The payroll maker traces the records of the contractual and job order employees whose deductions were not remitted to SSS due to non-registration with SSS and refund the same amount to them, if warranted.

 
Moving forward, the LGU facilitates the SSS registration of the LGU’s contractual and job order employees pursuant to the MOA entered into by the LGU.

The contractor and other persons liable to the transactions refund the overprice amounting to ₱592,029.19. 



Likewise, the Municipal Engineer exercises his function by reviewing all contracts, price estimates to avoid suspensions and disallowance in audit.

The Municipal Accountant determines the cause of the negative balance and prepares the necessary adjusting journal entries in order to correct the negative balance in the SL Barangay Share of RPT under the Due to LGUs account. 


Further, the Municipal Accountant reviews transactions before recording to ensure correctness and prevent existence of negative balances of accounts.

The management initiates action for the recovery of the amount disallowed. 

The Municipal Treasurer withholds payment of any money due from persons concerned to be applied in satisfaction of their indebtedness.

The audit team once again recommends that agency apply for a one-time cleansing, as outlined in detail in COA Circular No. 2020-006. To provide an overview, the circular generally mandates the updating of records and the conduct of an inventory taking to produce the following outputs.

List of PPEs “found at station” (Annex B). These are PPEs not included in the inventory working papers but there is reasonable basis to consider the same as owned by the agency. (Item 6.2.7)
Actions to be taken:
These items will be included in the inventory count and tagged with property stickers. (Item 6.2.7)
The accounting unit shall take up the necessary accounting entries to recognize them and prepare corresponding PPE Ledger Cards. (Item 6.3.2(a))
List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C). These are PPEs included in the inventory working papers but are not found during physical count (Item 6.2.8)
Actions to be taken:
The accounting unit shall take up the necessary accounting entries to recognize loss of PPE, and to set up corresponding receivables from the concerned accountable officer (Item 6.3.2(b)). Moreover, necessary accounting entries will also be taken up to write/drop off remaining balances of amounts lumped under “Unreconciled SL”, “Reconciling SL” for PPEs and the like, which were created to facilitate the conversion of accounts from the Old Government Accounting System to the New Government Accounting System. (Item 6.3.2(e))
Those for which accountability could not be established despite exhaustion of all diligent efforts, authority for derecognition thereof from the books of accounts may be requested from the COA (Item 7.11(d)) provided they have already exceeded their estimated useful life (Item 8.2(a)). Pending the grant of authority by the COA, the said PPEs shall remain in the books of accounts.
The agency should have taken the steps mentioned above, considering that PPE constitutes a significant portion of the agency's assets. As of December 31, 2024, the PPE had a total balance of ₱318,892,044.50, net of depreciation, as detailed below.
Due to the non-observance of the procedures as contained in COA Circular No. 2020-006 as well as noted deficiencies on PPE balances and properties, the accuracy of the reported PPE book balance cannot be ascertained.
We recommended that management prioritize updating all PPE-related records and conduct an inventory taking, after which reconciliation, as outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006, shall be made in order to arrive at the accurate PPE balance.

We recommended that a reconciliation be provided as an addition to the Notes to the Financial Statements to monitor developments in the old balances, the rationale for any additions, and the status of relief requests.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant send confirmation letters from the creditors to validate the existence of the payables and verify the same with the supporting documents used in booking up the said payables.  If non-existence of payable is warranted, revert the payables which have remained outstanding for a period of two (2) years or more.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances of reciprocal accounts and prepare necessary adjusting entries thereof. Any balances which require more time to reconcile like inherited balances shall be disclosed in the notes to the financial statements.

We recommended the following courses of action:

The Office of the Municipal Engineer coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Accountant as to the status of implementation of the projects and programs of each grants/donation.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant trace the sources of the accounts listed under the Due to NGAs and determine their respective status of implementation.
Liquidate all implemented and/or completed projects and submit the necessary supporting documents to the source agency.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant shall book the corresponding asset account for completed projects and recognize income through Prior Year Adjustments.
For excess funds, the same should be returned to the source agency as soon as possible.

We recommended that, henceforth, management ensure the availability of the SB-approved AIP on or before June 7 by initiating the formulation of the AIP earlier, no later than the month of May, and utilizing the MDC meeting as the appropriate venue for deliberating on the identification of priority development projects for the Municipality.

We recommended that management increase the fidelity bond of the Municipal Treasurer to adequately cover the maximum cash accountability from cash collections, as well as any other accountabilities associated with her position.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the journal entry to close inter-agency payables immediately upon liquidation.

We recommended that management reconcile the lumped amounts and request a write-off for those accounts that qualify as dormant. For the remaining receivables, we recommend sending demand letters to facilitate collection.

The Inventory Committee prioritizes and ensures full implementation of the “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.
 
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO/Municipal Treasurer maintain and update the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) and Property Cards (PC) for proper monitoring and reconciliation.

The Municipal Accountant maintains checklists for various types of transactions based from the documentary requirements prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other laws and regulations, and attach the applicable checklist in every disbursement voucher to guide the concerned officials/employees on the minimum documentary requirements needed for the transactions.

The management adheres to the rules and regulations stated under COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2009-002 and submit to the office of the auditor the necessary documents required for the audit team to give timely auditorial procedures as needed.

The management discontinues the practice of granting cash advances to accountable officers in amounts exceeding their corresponding maximum cash accountability. Otherwise, the management increase the fidelity bond of the AO to cater to the maximum cash accountability per cash advance drawn.

The management requires the Property Officer to maintain and regularly update the Stock Cards to account for all medicines and medical supplies, and to submit a monitoring report to the Accounting Department at the end of each quarter for reconciliation.
  
The Property and Accounting Departments also reconcile any discrepancies noted during the quarterly reconciliations of Inventory/Property Monitoring and Accounting Records to present reliable balances in the financial statements.

The management provides adequate, convenient, safe and well-ventilated storage facilities for medicines or a temporary storage facility if permanent storage facility is still unavailable for proper safe-keeping of all medicines and medical supplies that are not yet distributed to the end-users.

Likewise, the management provides an explanation on the slow distribution of the medicines considering that this is an identified project to address the adverse effect of Typhoon Odette.

The Municipal Treasurer submits every beginning of the year to the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year pursuant to Section 20 of the NGAS Manual 

The Municipal Treasurer reconciles the variance noted and avoid the improper set-up of RPT/SET Receivable in the succeeding periods.

The Municipal Accountant reverts the adjustment made pending receipt of the correct and complete report of the Municipal Agriculturist and any adjustment made shall be duly supported with pertinent documents.
The Municipal Treasurer releases timely to the barangays the corresponding amount due them pursuant to Section 271 of RA No. 7160 and Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382, dated July 3, 1992.

The LDRRMC prepares the LDRRMFIP following the COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, by incorporating the detailed plan for the prior years’ unexpended balances recorded in the Trust Liabilities – LDRRM. 

Further, the Council ensures that disbursements shall only be made out of the unexpended balance if included in the revised LDRRMFIP approved by the Sangguniang Bayan

The Inventory Committee and Accounting Department identify the properties subject for disposal. 

The Property Officer together with the Disposal Committee processes the documents/forms for disposal of unserviceable properties and submits to the COA Auditor pursuant to the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System, and Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

 Further, the Municipal Accountant, upon disposal, drops the cost of the PPE from the books to reflect the accurate value of PPE in the financial statements.

The Municipal Treasurer at the beginning of the year furnishes a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant. The Municipal Accountant shall set up Real Property Tax Receivable based on the amount on the list at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator facilitate the evaluation of the status of implementation of the funds received from National Government Agencies and other LGUs. Submit the liquidation of all implemented projects that remained unliquidated. Further, return the balances of those liquidated financial assistance to the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended that management direct the payroll maker to trace the records of the contractual and job order employees whose deductions were not remitted to SSS due to non-registration with SSS and refund the same amount to them, if warranted. Moving forward, the LGU has to facilitate the SSS registration of the LGU’s contractual and job order employees pursuant to the MOA entered into by the LGU.

We recommended that the management direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to adopt measures to strengthen the handling of cash tickets by conducting monthly reconciliation of the accountable forms. Require daily remittances of cash tickets and ensure that balances per RCD be checked against the actual cash ticket in the possession of the collectors.

We recommended that management ensure the proper implementation of the centralized ICT network by undertaking measures to address the reasons as to why the offices opted not to disconnect with their existing internet connections. Moreover, coordinate with the service provider and evaluate the viability of the centralized ICT network so that the intended purpose is guaranteed, and lessen subscription costs of the ICT.

We recommended that management direct the Sangguniang Bayan to stop hiring job order and contract of service personnel and to authorize the Local Chief Executive of hiring these workers pursuant to Sections 77, 444(b)(1)(vi) and 22(c) of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive evaluate the necessity of hiring the COS and determine whether CSC laws were not being circumvented to avoid any disallowances in audit.  Further, consider the creation of the Municipal Administrator as a plantilla position in accordance with the rules, laws and regulations set forth in the Local Government Code. Finally, stop the hiring of COS whose works are already performed by regular agency personnel

We recommended that that the LDRMMC formulate plans that are specific and based on the assessment of the needs of the local government unit as far as disaster management is concerned, otherwise, secure first authority from the local sanggunian before any changes in the investment plan particularly when a specific item is being identified to be procured in relation to disaster prevention and management. Any similar future transactions bereft of local sanggunian authorization will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that management require all officials and employees concerned and responsible in the preparation, approval of trip tickets and authorization of passenger/s to ensure that driver’s trip ticket is duly and properly accomplished and approved before undertaking the trip. Also, require the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travel for each vehicle and the Fuel Consumption Report, and submit the same to the auditor for evaluation of the reasonableness of fuel consumption.

We recommend that the management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Agriculturist to make further investigation on the unaccounted difference caused by the conflicting information reported in the inventory records of the Municipal Agriculturist as to the nature and type of Dispersal Program as against the disclosures in the 2011 Notes to FS pertaining to Breeding Stocks account.

Direct the Municipal Agriculturist to coordinate with the Senior Citizen Council (SCC), the recipient and in-charge of the Dispersal Program to apprise its status from its inception sometime in 2009 and submit list of beneficiaries of the program.

Secure memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/Contracts pertaining to any Dispersal Program and verify if all the provisions stated therein are followed, and if payment had been required from the beneficiaries obtain proof of such payment as basis for any warranted adjustment in the account Breeding Stocks.

Require submission of the Request for Relief from Accountability for deaths or any form of losses of breeding stocks as basis for derecognizing the value of breeding stocks and recording the corresponding receivable account from the beneficiary pursuant to Section 268 of GAM for LGUs. Close the receivable account to Loss of Assets account only when COA grants the request for relief from accountability.

Require the Municipal Accountant to revert the adjustment made pending receipt of the correct and complete report of the Municipal Agriculturist and any adjustment made shall be duly supported with pertinent documents.

We recommend that the management direct the GAD Focal Person to regularly assess the status of implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget and to timely and properly evaluate those activities that are not yet fully implemented before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the intended beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

We recommend that Management direct the officials and employees concerned to refund the unauthorized excess payment of the incentives given to them and stop the practice of granting the same in excess of the allowed ₱10,000.00 per employee to avoid disallowance in the future.  Also, we recommend that Management observe the frugal and judicious means of spending government resources in line with the provisions of COA Circular No. 85-55A.

We recommend that henceforth management observe BIR rules on the withholding of any forms of taxes due to the government as required by revenue regulations and require the refund of the taxes not withheld from the payment of the professional fees.

We recommend that Management demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter and uphold observance of accountability and transparency of all government transactions.

We recommended that management require the Inventory Committee to prioritize and complete the physical inventory within a reasonable period in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. Also, management is enjoined to ensure the full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in the same circular.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to trace the items under the Road Networks account, determine the date of acquisition, and reflect the unrecognized depreciation in the year of acquisition to bring the accumulated depreciation to its accurate balance.
We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting journal entries for the computed understatement of depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation as follows:

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to determine the cause of the negative balance and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries in order to correct the negative balance in the SL Barangay Share of RPT under the Due to LGUs account. Further, review transactions before recording to ensure correctness and prevent existence of negative balances of accounts.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all payables are recorded at year-end pursuant to Annex A (13) of COA Circular No. 2001-005 dated October 30, 2001. Moreover, all offices concerned have to report all purchased items already delivered and accepted to the accounting office at year-end for proper accounting. The end-users must also observe the timely processing of papers to prevent delays in payments.

We recommended that management direct the CMCH Hospital Administrator to provide the Municipal Accountant with the monthly computation for Professional Fees which forms part in the setting up of receivable from Philhealth. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant has to use appropriate entries in the recording and payment of Professional Fees.

We recommended that the management require the Municipal Accountant to revert the accounts payable without valid supporting documents and outstanding for more than two years to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 98 of PD 1445 and DBM-COA Joint Circular No. 99-6.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to analyze the Due to Philhealth account and determine the amounts withheld prior to employee’s registration with Philhealth for the current and prior years. Further, effect adjusting entries on the LGU share of the amounts withheld and refund the corresponding employee’s share to the employees concerned. Henceforth, the HRMO has to ensure that employees have their Philhealth registration number as part of their requirements prior to their appointment as regular employees.

We recommended that management require all officials and employees concerned and responsible in the preparation, approval of trip tickets and authorization of passenger/s to ensure that driver’s trip ticket is duly and properly accomplished and approved before undertaking the trip. Also, require the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travel for each vehicle and the Fuel Consumption Report, and submit the same to the auditor for evaluation of the reasonableness of fuel consumption.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to release timely to the barangays the corresponding amount due them pursuant to Section 271 of RA No. 7160 and Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382, dated July 3, 1992.

We recommended that management stop the practice of procuring office supplies on a staggered basis. Limit the use of alternative modes of procurement only when the circumstances warrant which fall within the provisions of Section 52 and Section 53 of RA 9184.  Henceforth, instruct the respective end-users of the different offices to meticulously and religiously plan the procurement of office supplies needed for the efficient operations of the LGU.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Engineer and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that the statements and documents submitted by the bidder are responsive to the demands of the projects, such as but not limited to the availability of key personnel and the equipment units pledged.

We recommended that management facilitate the revision/update of the revenue code pursuant to Section 191 of RA 7160 through revisiting articles and provisions of the Municipal’s existing Revenue Code series of 2011 considering present business environment towards the best interest of the public and the government.  We also recommend that enactment of the revised revenue code be made immediately so the potential increase in collections will be achieved to provide additional funds which can be used to finance programs and projects of LGU.

We recommended that management require the LDRRMC to prepare the LDRRMFIP following the COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, by incorporating the detailed plan for the prior years’ unexpended balances recorded in the Trust Liabilities – LDRRM. Further, ensure that disbursements shall only be made out of the unexpended balance if included in the revised LDRRMFIP approved by the Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended that management direct the LDRRMC and MBO to stop the practice of disbursing funds in excess of what was allocated within the year. Further, establish monitoring mechanisms in which programs, projects and activities are undertaken within the budget allocated and ensure that the fund for future disbursements is utilized properly to implement the programmed activities of the LGU.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to record the receipt of donated funds to its appropriate account, and the LDRRMO and MBO to incorporate it to the LDRRMFIP in accordance with Section 5.2.4 of COA Circular 2012-002. Also, reclassify the donated funds from Due to LGUs to Trust Liabilities – LDRRM as follows:
Due to LGUs				₱78,680.00
Trust Liabilities – LDRRM		₱78,680.00
Moreover, the utilization of such fund has to be included in the Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.

We recommended that management direct the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Engineer to establish adequate measures in safeguarding the assets of the LGU. Determine a delivery period that would be sufficient to cover the construction schedule to minimize the risks brought by the handling and storing of materials, particularly those with high risk of moisture and bio-deterioration. Moreover, delivery of items has to be properly monitored to ensure that projects are implemented as scheduled.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant observe appropriate use of accounts to present fairly the financial statements of the LGU. Expenses for the transportation of LSIs have to be recorded as Transportation and Delivery Expenses, rather than Food Supplies Expenses pursuant to the account description provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is using the appropriate account titles pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer take up the necessary adjustments in the cashbooks to reconcile both the cashbook and the subsidiary ledger balances. Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their records at least quarterly pursuant to Section 96 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s cashbook already made the appropriate adjustments dated march 8, 2021. There is already an assigned accounting staff to regularly reconcile the cashbooks and subsidiary ledgers on a quarterly basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management designate qualified personnel other than the Municipal Accountant to temporarily perform the functions of the MBO, pending the appointment of the new Municipal Budget Officer to maintain proper segregation of duties pursuant to Section 50 of GAAM and Section 10.1.3 of ICSPPS. The Internal Audit Services is tasked to ensure the adequacy and effectiveness of the internal control system within the municipality. Further, prioritize the hiring of the Municipal Budget Officer the soonest time possible.
observations.managementAction: The management already hired and appointed a new Municipal Budget Officer effective march 1, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the department heads of the health services sectors to include a certification on the Purchase Requests for all drugs and medicines needs in accordance with E.O. 49. Henceforth, the BAC has to ensure that certifications are included in all P.R.s submitted for procurement of drugs and medicines; otherwise, future similar deficiencies noted will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) complied the certification requirements set by EO No. 49 for all succeeding procurement for drugs and medicines.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide explanation on why no contract was entered into by and between the LGU and the transport provider. Moreover, we recommend that henceforth management observe BIR rules on the withholding of any forms of taxes due to the government as required by revenue regulations and require the refund of the taxes not withheld from the payment of the transportation expenses.
observations.managementAction: The management justified that the LGU had scouted for the means of transportation to cater the return of the LSIs. Where no other provider is willing to transport and with the scare of carrying individuals who might be infected but he virus and where time is of the essence there is no time to have a contract be executed between the LGU and the transport provider.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that distribution of PHIC reimbursements particularly professional fees has to be aligned with the DOH AO 2016-003. The payment of the benefit has to be shared based on the pooling scheme prescribed by the DOH. Moreover, ensure that the health care facility abide the provisions of DOH Administrative Order and other issuances from PHIC.  Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all benefits given and received by each employee entitled to claim such benefit are shown individually on the payroll.
observations.managementAction: The management will comply with the administrative order for future professional fees claim.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the official concerned to let the pensioners acknowledge receipt of the benefit by indicating their signature or thumbmark on the spaces provided on the payrolls to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit. In cases where the pensioner cannot personally claim the benefit, an authorization letter must be secured first by the immediate member whom the pensioner has authorized before they can claim the pension.
observations.managementAction: The Disbursing Officer had the payroll of the beneficiaries acknowledged by the recipients of the social pension and submitted the same to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct those responsible for the procurement to submit written justification for the noted price variance and defying the DOH Memorandum Nos. 2020-0058 and 2020-0014A that were issued at the height of the pandemic. Henceforth, require the end-users and BAC to abide and be updated with rules and regulations relate to COVID-19 for the prevention of abuses and corruption in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted its justification that, the price surge of supplies was very prevalent, resulting from a lack of supply and high demand. Due to the urgency of the need for the supplies, the LGU had no other choice but to accept the lowest price offer even if it exceeded the maximum price.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to management with the assistance of the MPDC that projects identified have to be implemented within the year to deliver the intended benefits to the people and to get the maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices of materials in order to address the needs of the community. observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace the transactions that resulted in the recorded balance of the Government Equity and provide adjustments to correct the misstatement of the equity and other affected accounts in the current and prior years pursuant to the applicable accounting standards.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant observe appropriate use of accounts to present fairly the financial statements of the LGU. Expenses for the transportation of LSIs have to be recorded as Transportation and Delivery Expenses, rather than Food Supplies Expenses pursuant to the account description provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer take up the necessary adjustments in the cashbooks to reconcile both the cashbook and the subsidiary ledger balances. Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their records at least quarterly pursuant to Section 96 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

We recommend that the management require the officers concerned to explain on the deficiencies noted particularly on the reimbursement of expenditures duly paid and sufficiently covered with an appropriation thru fund transfer. We further recommend that management request authority from the source agency to utilize the financial assistance for other beneficial school-related projects/programs/activities in the municipality.

We recommend that the management designate qualified personnel other than the Municipal Accountant to temporarily perform the functions of the MBO, pending the appointment of the new Municipal Budget Officer to maintain proper segregation of duties pursuant to Section 50 of GAAM and Section 10.1.3 of ICSPPS. The Internal Audit Services is tasked to ensure the adequacy and effectiveness of the internal control system within the municipality. Further, prioritize the hiring of the Municipal Budget Officer the soonest time possible.

We recommend that the management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Agriculturist to make further investigation on the unaccounted difference caused by the conflicting information reported in the inventory records of the Municipal Agriculturist as to the nature and type of Dispersal Program as against the disclosures in the 2011 Notes to FS pertaining to Breeding Stocks account.
Direct the Municipal Agriculturist to coordinate with the Senior Citizen Council (SCC), the recipient and in-charge of the Dispersal Program to apprise its status from its inception sometime in 2009 and submit list of beneficiaries of the program.
Secure memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/Contracts pertaining to any Dispersal Program and verify if all the provisions stated therein are followed, and if payment had been required from the beneficiaries obtain proof of such payment as basis for any warranted adjustment in the account Breeding Stocks.
Require submission of the Request for Relief from Accountability for deaths or any form of losses of breeding stocks as basis for derecognizing the value of breeding stocks and recording the corresponding receivable account from the beneficiary pursuant to Section 268 of GAM for LGUs. Close the receivable account to Loss of Assets account only when COA grants the request for relief from accountability.
Require the Municipal Accountant to revert the adjustment made pending receipt of the correct and complete report of the Municipal Agriculturist and any adjustment made shall be duly supported with pertinent documents.

We recommend that management require the department heads of the health services sectors to include a certification on the Purchase Requests for all drugs and medicines needs in accordance with E.O. 49. Henceforth, the BAC has to ensure that certifications are included in all P.R.s submitted for procurement of drugs and medicines; otherwise, future similar deficiencies noted will be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that management provide explanation on why no contract was entered into by and between the LGU and the transport provider. Moreover, we recommend that henceforth management observe BIR rules on the withholding of any forms of taxes due to the government as required by revenue regulations and require the refund of the taxes not withheld from the payment of the transportation expenses.

We recommend that distribution of PHIC reimbursements particularly professional fees has to be aligned with the DOH AO 2016-003. The payment of the benefit has to be shared based on the pooling scheme prescribed by the DOH. Moreover, ensure that the health care facility abide the provisions of DOH Administrative Order and other issuances from PHIC.  Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all benefits given and received by each employee entitled to claim such benefit are shown individually on the payroll.

We recommend that management immediately stop the practice of charging to SEF the transactions outside the scope for which this fund was established and observe the proper utilization thereof pursuant to the provisions of Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG to attain the government's goal in operation and maintenance of elementary and secondary schools. Moreover, require the refund of the improper charge to the SEF.

We recommend that management require the official concerned to let the pensioners acknowledge receipt of the benefit by indicating their signature or thumbmark on the spaces provided on the payrolls to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit. In cases where the pensioner cannot personally claim the benefit, an authorization letter must be secured first by the immediate member whom the pensioner has authorized before they can claim the pension.

We recommend that management direct those responsible for the procurement to submit written justification for the noted price variance and defying the DOH Memorandum Nos. 2020-0058 and 2020-0014A that were issued at the height of the pandemic. Henceforth, require the end-users and BAC to abide and be updated with rules and regulations relate to COVID-19 for the prevention of abuses and corruption in the procurement process.

While non-implementation of some programs and activities is understood, in the event when restrictions are lifted, the management is encouraged to maximize the utilization of the GAD budget so that activities, programs and other relevant issues relevant to GAD will be addressed and prioritized.

While it is understood that the LGU was not able to implement some projects due to the pandemic, it is recommended that projects, programs and activities identified have to be enforced to deliver intended benefits to its constituents and to achieve maximum utilization of the fund. Delayed project implementation can lead to various constraints that will ultimately cause their non-implementation, such as price escalation of supplies and materials for the projects; impracticability or non-feasibility of projects due to the changing times and priorities. On the other hand, the non-implementation of projects will defeat the government's commitment towards the constituents and deprive the people of the intended benefits.

We recommend that management secure and comply the pertinent documentary requirements related to the purchase of a parcel of land as enumerated in COA Circular 2012-001 to ensure validity of the claim and protect the interest and welfare of the government, otherwise the transaction will be suspended in audit.  Further, ensure that the ownership of the acquired lot be transferred to the LGU through processing of the transfer documents in order to protect the interest of the municipality. Finally, secure the surety bond from the seller/previous owner of the lot purchased.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommend that management:
Create a committee or assign personnel to conduct the annual physical inventory of its PPE, prepare the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and reconcile said report with the Property and Accounting Department’s records to establish the correct value of the PPE in the financial statements. The RPCPPE shall be submitted to COA.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and staff to prepare and maintain an updated PPE Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each class of PPE to record promptly the acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, depreciation, impairment, issue/transfers/disposal, repair history and other information about the property and reconcile the same with the Property Officer’s records and the RCPPE, any discrepancies shall be reconciled.
Instruct the Property Officer to prepare and maintain an updated Property Cards (PC) for each class of PPE and reconcile the same with the accountant’s records and the RCPPE, any discrepancies shall be reconciled. Likewise, adopt the use of Property Tags/Stickers on all old and newly acquired property and equipment as an effective control on the use of government property and equipment. The Inventory Tags/Stickers should be attached to the newly acquired equipment before these are distributed to the intended end-users.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the management instruct the Inventory Committee to determine all donated properties in each department of the agency. Create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the donated properties in the absence of transfer/donation documents in order for the Municipal Accountant to have a basis for the cost of the property to be recorded in the books of accounts. Likewise, require the issuance of ARE/PAR for donated items issued to recipient or custodian to establish accountability over the donated property. Following the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2004-003 dated October 4, 2004, corresponding depreciation must also be considered after the elapse of time since the acquisition thereon. Prior years’ depreciation should be taken up as Prior Years Adjustment to be closed to Government Equity and current year’s depreciation should be charged to Depreciation Expense account.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Inventory Committee and Accounting Department to identify the properties subject for disposal. Direct the Property Officer together with the Disposal Committee to process the documents/forms for disposal of unserviceable properties and submit to the COA Auditor pursuant to the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System, and Section 79 of PD No. 1445. Further, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant, upon disposal, drop the cost of the PPE from the books to reflect the accurate value of PPE in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Agricultural Officer prepare and maintain the ledger cards and stock/property cards, respectively, for the Breeding Stocks and reconcile their balances regularly. Further, conduct physical inventory of the breeding stocks and recommend the inclusion of the Municipal Agricultural Officer as member of the Inventory Team. The inventory report shall be submitted not later than January 31 of each year.
If any discrepancies will be noted between the inventory report and accounting records verify and investigate from the custodian of the livestock the causes/reasons and recommend for filing request for relief of accountability and disclose in the notes to financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant make an analysis of the accounts with negative balances, trace to prior years the cause of occurrence of negative beginning balance and make necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the accounts. Further, review transactions before recording to correctly identify the nature of the transaction and ensure correctness in recording to prevent existence of negative balances of accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant (MA) record purchases of Accountable Forms in the AF, Plates and Stickers Inventory Account and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the monthly Inventory Report of Accountable Forms (IRAF). The report will show in detail all issuances of accountable forms to the collectors and sales to the barangay which the MA will use as basis for the recording of issuance of AF by crediting Accountable Forms, Plates and Stickers Inventory at month end.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant locate and trace the causes of said unreconciled balances of RPT and SET receivables and effect the adjustments/corrections of the affected accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant observe appropriate use of accounts to present fairly the financial statements of the LGU. Registration fees from trainings/conventions and seminars/workshops have to be recorded as Training expenses, rather than Traveling Expenses pursuant to the account description provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management secure and comply the pertinent documentary requirements related to the purchase of a parcel of land as enumerated in COA Circular 2012-001 to ensure validity of the claim and protect the interest and welfare of the government, otherwise the transaction will be suspended in audit.  Further, ensure that the ownership of the acquired lot be transferred to the LGU through processing of the transfer documents in order to protect the interest of the municipality. Finally, secure the surety bond from the seller/previous owner of the lot purchased.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to determine available funds from savings of the current year’s PS for possible appropriation of the deficient hazard benefit due to the MHO. With the concurrence of the Sanggunian Bayan (SB), taking into consideration of the PS limitation, supplemental budget may be prepared for the payment of the differential of the underprovided hazard pay. Subsequently, the SB has to enact an ordinance for the appropriation of the correct hazard pay pursuant to DBM-DOH Joint Circular No.1 series of 2016. Further, it is also recommended that the Municipal Accountant be updated with latest rules and regulations for its proper application so that benefits due shall be rightfully availed by concerned officials and employees.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the management require the payroll maker to reflect the full amount of laundry allowance of ₱150.00 in the PHWs payroll regardless of the actual work rendered. Further, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant check if the payroll prepared is correct and in accordance with the established rules and regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management instruct the Property Officer to maintain an efficient property management by following the prescribed procedures in property recording and maintain accurate property records and regularly renew Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR).

Recommendation:
In view hereof, we recommend that the management direct the GAD Focal Person to regularly assess the status of implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget and to timely and properly evaluate those activities that are not yet fully implemented before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the intended beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that all collections be remitted intact and daily to avoid misuse of
government funds. Likewise, management have to call the attention of the above
collectors and remind their duties and responsibilities; otherwise, they be relieved from
the collecting function of various income payments and be replaced by efficient and
reliable collectors to ensure safety and proper accountability of government funds.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
adjust/reclassify the aforecited accounts to its appropriate account in conformity with
the New Chart of Accounts under the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards
(PPSAS).

We recommend that the concerned department head refrain from certifying in
the Obligation Request the validity of supporting documents for the goods or services
procured are valid, proper and legal when the bases for recognition of assets, expenses
and payables are not yet available. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal
Accountant refrain from booking up the Accounts Payable in the books when there are
no valid bases yet to do such. Finally, effect the reversion of the accounts payable that
lacked the necessary documents for payment and with no identified claimants in order
to present fairly the expense and payable accounts.

We recommend that the accountant record the receivables from the Philhealth
representing the hospital charges and professional fees in order to have fair
presentation of the financial statements.

We recommend that management:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary accounts entry to effect
reconciliation of the affected accounts.
2. For transactions paid from certain funds which are not proper charges to the fund,
require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a check to immediately refund
erroneous charging and be cautious in issuing checks.

Require the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation for all depreciable
assets booked under the Property, Plant and Equipment in order to present accurate
.
balances of the assets in the financial statements of the municipality

We recommend that management conduct immediate verification on the
deficiency of the remittance of the taxes, premiums and loan amortizations of officials
and employees to protect the finances of the local government unit. Further, require
the municipal accountant to thoroughly check all remittances and trace back to the
respective payrolls to ensure accurate figures remitted to the different government
agencies to protect officials and employees who have been religiously paying their
indebtedness.

We recommend that management conduct immediate verification on the
deficiency noted on the remittance of the loan amortizations of employees and remit
efficiently what was dutifully paid by them. Finally, require the accounting to reconcile
the payments made with the GSIS records to rectify the erroneous deduction and
prevent collection of arrears from the affected employee.

We recommend that henceforth management observe BIR rules on the
withholding of any forms of taxes due to the government as required by revenue
regulations and require the refund of the taxes not withheld from the payment of the
professional fees.

We recommend that management prioritize and consider filling –up the vacant
positions by hiring new employees or expedite promotion of deserving employees to
enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of
the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents as well as to
provide equal job opportunities to the community.

We recommend that management provide a venue to discuss with the municipal
employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its employees are able to receive a
minimum of ₱4,000 take home pay per month for them to maintain self-respect and
dignity as employees of the government, in pursuance of Section 7 of the 2017 GAA and
years thereafter.
Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO monitor
payroll deductions and refuse to sign certifications for application of loans should an
employee net pay fall below ₱4,000.00.

We recommend that future financial assistance for barangay developmental
projects be released only once a MOA is made between both parties. The MOA has to
include conditions that in case of failure to implement within a specific period of time,
the barangay has to return the amount received.
Further, follow-up has to be made by the concerned municipal
officials/department for those barangays with unliquidated financial assistance and
compel them to send liquidation reports immediately, otherwise, future request for
project funding be denied because of the unliquidated financial assistance.

We recommend that the repairs, maintenance and replacement of parts of
government vehicles be supported with Report of Waste Materials (RWM) so that the
payments for the related transactions are valid and proper.

We recommend that the travel expenses amounting P3,564.50 be refunded by
the concerned official and require SB Rengel to file application for leave for two (2)
days: November 17-18, 2017 because the travel is personal in nature and please
furnishes a copy of the Application for Leave to this office.

Auditor’s Rejoinder
Republic Act 9184 was promulgated to systematize the procurement process of the
government, promote transparency and increase operational efficiency and effectiveness thus
every provision contained have to be strictly adhered to. Circumventing the Procurement
Law is a grave act that any BAC will commit. Time delays could have been avoided if there
was proper planning and monitoring of projects by focal persons, without compromising the
rules and regulations of RA 9184. Further, transacting with a middlemen though it quoted a
low price is not an assurance that prices offered were most advantageous to the government.
Notice of Disallowance was also issued for the items that were found out to be
overpriced based on the quotation of the supplier.
